rationale: Educ. Code §44.031 governs school district purchasing contract methods and best-value criteria. It contains no reference to homestead exemptions, property tax appraisal, or Tax Code §11.13(b). The proposed change is wholly unrelated to this statute.
quote: all school district contracts for the purchase of goods and services, except contracts for the purchase of produce or vehicle fuel, valued at $100,000 or more in the aggregate for each 12-month period shall be made by the method, of the following methods, that provides the best value for the district
_note: No conflict. The proposed homestead exemption increase under Tax Code §11.13(b) does not intersect with school district procurement rules in Educ. Code §44.031.
